message:
fix: unify timestamp normalization across OpenCode connector, Cursor connector, and export paths

Three timestamp-handling bugs fixed by routing all timestamps through the
canonical parse_timestamp() normalizer:

1. OpenCode connector (src/connectors/opencode.rs):
   - Session started_at/ended_at and message created_at fields were using
     raw i64 values directly from OpenCode JSON without normalization
   - Added normalize_opencode_timestamp() helper that converts raw i64
     through parse_timestamp() to detect seconds-vs-milliseconds epoch
   - Applied to scan() session time bounds, load_messages() created_at,
     and test assertions (1733000000 → 1_733_000_000_000)

2. Cursor connector (src/connectors/cursor.rs):
   - Tab timestamps used as_i64() directly instead of parse_timestamp(),
     bypassing epoch-unit detection
   - Replaced ad-hoc started_at/ended_at tracking (first-message-sets-start,
     last-message-sets-end) with update_time_bounds() closure that correctly
     computes min/max across all messages regardless of encounter order

3. Export functions (src/lib.rs):
   - load_opencode_session_for_export(): normalized session_start/session_end
     through parse_timestamp(), tracks message_ts_min/message_ts_max instead
     of relying on sorted first/last message JSON fields for fallback bounds,
     omits "timestamp" key from message JSON when timestamp is absent instead
     of writing synthetic 0, sorts unknown-timestamp messages to end via
     i64::MAX sentinel
   - run_export() and run_export_html(): replaced inline
     msg.get("timestamp").and_then(|t| t.as_i64()) with new
     extract_message_timestamp() helper that probes timestamp in direct,
     message.timestamp, and payload.timestamp locations via parse_timestamp()
   - Added extract_message_timestamp() function with fallback chain:
     direct → nested message → nested payload

4. Tests added:
   - opencode_export_tests: verifies missing timestamps don't produce
     artificial 0 values and bounds come only from real timestamps
   - export_timestamp_tests: verifies extract_message_timestamp handles
     i64, ISO 8601 strings, numeric strings, nested message/payload shapes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
